mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over the 20-day forward return horizon.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 210, persistent inflation expectations compress real equity valuations and trigger risk-off sentiment, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 210.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals a broad-based collapse in consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary onset regime, a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% confirms labor market deterioration, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances during confirmed recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production (INDPRO) reflects immediate supply-side contraction and weak economic activity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO changes is positive or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over the 5-day forward return horizon.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) falls below 1.0%, it indicates severe economic stagnation, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The co-occurrence of high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 210) and rising unemployment (UNRATE > 4.5) creates a stagflationary environment that is strongly bearish, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and UNRATE has a positive coefficient or is statistically insignificant in predicting 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 4.0% while industrial production (INDPRO) is declining, the market anticipates aggressive Fed intervention (the 'Fed Put'), generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS < 4.0 and INDPRO is declining.
